name: Brute Force - Users Under Attack
desc: Detects accounts receiving more than 10 failed sign-in attempts. High failure counts from multiple IPs indicate targeted brute force attacks.
kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize
    FailedAttempts = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 5),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedAttempts > 10
| extend AttackType = case(
    UniqueIPs > 5, "Distributed Brute Force",
    FailedAttempts > 100, "Heavy Brute Force",
    "Standard Brute Force")
| order by FailedAttempts desc

name: Successful Login After Failed Attempts
desc: Identifies accounts that successfully signed in after a series of failures. This pattern strongly indicates credential compromise.
kql:
let FailedAccounts = SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailCount = count(), LastFail = max(TimeGenerated) by UserPrincipalName
| where FailCount > 5;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| join kind=inner (FailedAccounts) on UserPrincipalName
| where TimeGenerated > LastFail
| where datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, LastFail) <= 30
| extend Country = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion)
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, Country, AppDisplayName, FailCount
| order by FailCount desc

name: Password Spray Detection
desc: Detects IPs attempting to sign in across 10+ different user accounts per hour. Password spraying avoids lockout by trying one password against many users.
kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize
    TargetedUsers = dcount(UserPrincipalName),
    Attempts = count(),
    Users = make_set(UserPrincipalName, 10)
    by IPAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where TargetedUsers >= 10
| extend SprayIntensity = case(
    TargetedUsers > 50, "CRITICAL - Mass Spray",
    TargetedUsers > 20, "HIGH",
    "MEDIUM")
| order by TargetedUsers desc

name: Password Spray Success (Compromise)
desc: Finds successful sign-ins from IPs previously identified as password spray sources. A successful login from a spray IP means at least one account is compromised.
kql:
let SprayIPs = SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize TargetedUsers = dcount(UserPrincipalName) by IPAddress
| where TargetedUsers >= 10
| project IPAddress;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| where IPAddress in (SprayIPs)
| extend Country = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion)
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, Country, AppDisplayName
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: MFA Fatigue / Bombing Detection
desc: Detects 5+ MFA push prompts to the same user within 10 minutes. Attackers repeatedly trigger MFA hoping the user accidentally approves.
kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == "50074"
| summarize
    MFAPrompts = count(),
    TimeSpan = datetime_diff('minute', max(TimeGenerated), min(TimeGenerated)),
    IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 5)
    by UserPrincipalName, bin(TimeGenerated, 10m)
| where MFAPrompts >= 5
| extend RiskLevel = case(
    MFAPrompts > 20, "CRITICAL - Heavy Bombing",
    MFAPrompts > 10, "HIGH",
    "MEDIUM")
| order by MFAPrompts desc

name: MFA Fatigue Success
desc: Identifies successful MFA completion following a fatigue/bombing attack. The user likely approved the push by mistake — immediate session revocation needed.
kql:
let MFABombedUsers = SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == "50074"
| summarize MFAPrompts = count() by UserPrincipalName, bin(TimeGenerated, 10m)
| where MFAPrompts >= 5
| project UserPrincipalName;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| where UserPrincipalName in (MFABombedUsers)
| where AuthenticationRequirement == "multiFactorAuthentication"
| extend Country = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion)
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, Country, AppDisplayName
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: AiTM Phishing / Token Replay
desc: Detects the same session used from multiple IP addresses, indicating stolen session token replay from attacker infrastructure after AiTM phishing.
kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| where isnotempty(OriginalRequestId)
| summarize
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 10),
    Countries = make_set(tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion), 5)
    by OriginalRequestId, UserPrincipalName
| where UniqueIPs > 1
| extend RiskLevel = case(
    UniqueIPs > 3, "CRITICAL - Multi-IP Token Replay",
    "HIGH - Investigate")
| order by UniqueIPs desc

name: Impossible Travel Detection
desc: Calculates travel speed between consecutive sign-ins. Speed >800 km/h with >500 km distance flags impossible travel indicating compromised credentials.
kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| extend Lat = todouble(LocationDetails.geoCoordinates.latitude)
| extend Lon = todouble(LocationDetails.geoCoordinates.longitude)
| where isnotempty(Lat) and isnotempty(Lon)
| order by UserPrincipalName, TimeGenerated
| serialize
| extend PrevLat = prev(Lat), PrevLon = prev(Lon), PrevTime = prev(TimeGenerated), PrevUser = prev(UserPrincipalName)
| where UserPrincipalName == PrevUser and isnotempty(PrevLat)
| extend TimeDiff = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, PrevTime)
| where TimeDiff > 0
| extend Distance_km = geo_distance_2points(Lon, Lat, PrevLon, PrevLat) / 1000
| extend Speed_kmh = Distance_km / TimeDiff * 60
| where Speed_kmh > 800 and Distance_km > 500
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, Distance_km = round(Distance_km, 0), Speed_kmh = round(Speed_kmh, 0), TimeDiff, IPAddress
| order by Speed_kmh desc

name: Distributed Brute Force / Botnet
desc: Detects 5+ different IPs targeting the same user account. Distributed attacks use botnets to avoid IP-based rate limiting.
kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize
    AttackingIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    Attempts = count(),
    IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 10),
    Countries = make_set(tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion), 5)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where AttackingIPs >= 5
| order by AttackingIPs desc

name: Low-and-Slow Password Spray
desc: Catches evasive spray attacks making fewer than 10 attempts per hour but persisting over 4+ hours. Designed to fly under traditional thresholds.
kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize
    AttemptCount = count(),
    UniqueUsers = dcount(UserPrincipalName),
    ActiveHours = dcount(bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by IPAddress
| where UniqueUsers >= 5 and ActiveHours >= 4 and AttemptCount / ActiveHours < 10
| extend Duration_hours = datetime_diff('hour', LastSeen, FirstSeen)
| order by UniqueUsers desc

name: Anonymous IP / VPN / Tor Detection
desc: Identifies sign-ins flagged with anonymized IP risk events. Tor exit nodes and anonymous VPNs are commonly used by attackers.
kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where RiskEventTypes_V2 has_any ("anonymizedIPAddress", "maliciousIPAddress")
| extend Country = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion)
| summarize
    Events = count(),
    IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 5),
    Countries = make_set(Country, 5),
    Apps = make_set(AppDisplayName, 5)
    by UserPrincipalName, RiskLevelDuringSignIn
| order by Events desc

name: First-Time Country Sign-In
desc: Compares current 7-day sign-in locations against a 30-day baseline. New countries are flagged as anomalous.
kql:
let Baseline = SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated between (ago(30d) .. ago(7d))
| where ResultType == "0"
| extend Country = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion)
| distinct UserPrincipalName, Country;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| extend Country = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion)
| where isnotempty(Country)
| join kind=leftanti (Baseline) on UserPrincipalName, Country
| summarize
    Events = count(),
    IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 5),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName, Country
| order by Events desc

name: Users Without MFA
desc: Lists users authenticating with single-factor only. These accounts are the easiest targets and should be prioritized for MFA enforcement.
kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| where AuthenticationRequirement == "singleFactorAuthentication"
| summarize
    SingleFactorLogins = count(),
    IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 5),
    Apps = make_set(AppDisplayName, 5),
    LastLogin = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| order by SingleFactorLogins desc

name: Legacy Authentication Usage
desc: Detects usage of legacy protocols (IMAP, POP, SMTP, ActiveSync) that bypass MFA. Attackers target these protocols specifically.
kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ClientAppUsed has_any ("IMAP", "POP", "SMTP", "Exchange ActiveSync", "Other clients", "Authenticated SMTP")
| summarize
    LegacyLogins = count(),
    Protocols = make_set(ClientAppUsed, 5),
    Apps = make_set(AppDisplayName, 5),
    LastUsed = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| order by LegacyLogins desc

name: Z-Score Statistical Anomaly
desc: Calculates Z-score for each user's daily sign-in volume against their 30-day baseline. Z-score >3 means statistically significant deviation.
kql:
let Baseline = SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated between (ago(30d) .. ago(1d))
| summarize DailyCount = count() by UserPrincipalName, bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)
| summarize AvgDaily = avg(DailyCount), StdDev = stdev(DailyCount) by UserPrincipalName;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| summarize TodayCount = count() by UserPrincipalName
| join kind=inner (Baseline) on UserPrincipalName
| where StdDev > 0
| extend ZScore = round((TodayCount - AvgDaily) / StdDev, 2)
| where ZScore > 3
| project UserPrincipalName, TodayCount, AvgDaily = round(AvgDaily, 0), StdDev = round(StdDev, 1), ZScore
| order by ZScore desc

name: Botnet IPs - Successful Logins
desc: Cross-references IPs from failed attack patterns with successful sign-ins. A successful login from a known attack IP is a high-confidence compromise.
kql:
let AttackIPs = SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailCount = count(), TargetedUsers = dcount(UserPrincipalName) by IPAddress
| where FailCount > 50 or TargetedUsers > 5
| project IPAddress;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| where IPAddress in (AttackIPs)
| extend Country = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion)
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, Country, AppDisplayName
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Threat Intel IP Correlation
desc: Joins sign-in logs with Sentinel ThreatIntelligenceIndicator table. Requires TI data connector enabled. Sentinel-only query.
kql:
// NOTE: This query works in Sentinel only (ThreatIntelligenceIndicator table)
let TIIPs = ThreatIntelligenceIndicator
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where isnotempty(NetworkIP)
| distinct NetworkIP;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where IPAddress in (TIIPs)
| extend Country = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion)
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, Country, AppDisplayName, ResultType
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Off-Hours & Weekend Activity
desc: Flags sign-in activity outside business hours (before 7am/after 7pm) and on weekends. Unusual timing combined with other indicators can confirm compromise.
kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| extend Hour = datetime_part("Hour", TimeGenerated)
| extend DayOfWeek = dayofweek(TimeGenerated)
| extend IsOffHours = case(
    DayOfWeek >= 5d, "Weekend",
    Hour < 7 or Hour > 19, "After Hours",
    "Business Hours")
| where IsOffHours != "Business Hours"
| summarize
    Events = count(),
    IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 5),
    Apps = make_set(AppDisplayName, 5)
    by UserPrincipalName, IsOffHours
| where Events > 5
| order by Events desc

name: Attack Chain Summary (MITRE)
desc: Maps detected attack patterns to MITRE ATT&CK phases: Reconnaissance through Lateral Movement. Provides a single view of active attack chains.

name: Token Theft - Geographic Impossibility
desc: Detects non-interactive sign-ins from 2+ countries within 120 minutes. Token theft allows attackers to use stolen refresh tokens from their own location.
kql:
AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| extend Country = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion)
| where isnotempty(Country)
| summarize
    Countries = make_set(Country, 10),
    CountryCount = dcount(Country),
    IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 10),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| extend TimeSpan = datetime_diff('minute', LastSeen, FirstSeen)
| where CountryCount >= 2 and TimeSpan <= 120
| order by CountryCount desc

name: Credential Stuffing - Single IP
desc: Identifies single IPs targeting 5+ different user accounts via non-interactive flows. Uses leaked username/password pairs from data breaches.
kql:
AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize
    TargetedUsers = dcount(UserPrincipalName),
    Attempts = count(),
    Users = make_set(UserPrincipalName, 10)
    by IPAddress
| where TargetedUsers >= 5
| order by TargetedUsers desc

name: Password Spray (Non-Interactive)
desc: Password spray targeting non-interactive sign-in endpoints. Attackers use automated tools against OAuth/token endpoints to avoid user-facing detections.
kql:
AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize
    TargetedUsers = dcount(UserPrincipalName),
    Attempts = count()
    by IPAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where TargetedUsers >= 10
| order by TargetedUsers desc

name: AI Shadow IT (ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini)
desc: Detects unapproved AI service usage by looking for non-interactive sign-ins to known AI application names. Helps enforce AI governance policies.
kql:
AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where AppDisplayName has_any ("ChatGPT", "OpenAI", "Claude", "Anthropic", "Gemini", "Google AI", "Copilot", "Midjourney", "Stable Diffusion", "Perplexity", "Jasper")
| summarize
    Events = count(),
    Users = make_set(UserPrincipalName, 10),
    UniqueUsers = dcount(UserPrincipalName)
    by AppDisplayName
| order by Events desc

name: Newly Detected Applications
desc: Compares current week's applications against a 90-day baseline. New applications could be shadow IT or malicious OAuth apps.
kql:
let BaselineApps = AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated between (ago(90d) .. ago(7d))
| distinct AppDisplayName;
AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where AppDisplayName !in (BaselineApps)
| summarize
    Events = count(),
    Users = make_set(UserPrincipalName, 5),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated)
    by AppDisplayName, AppId
| order by Events desc

name: Weekly Baseline Comparison
desc: Compares this week's non-interactive sign-in volume against previous week. >50% increase may indicate automated attacks.
kql:
let ThisWeek = AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize TW = count(), TW_Failed = countif(ResultType != "0");
let LastWeek = AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated between (ago(14d) .. ago(7d))
| summarize LW = count(), LW_Failed = countif(ResultType != "0");
ThisWeek | extend p = 1
| join kind=leftouter (LastWeek | extend p = 1) on p
| extend VolumeChange = round((todouble(TW) - LW) / max_of(LW, 1) * 100, 0)
| extend FailureChange = round((todouble(TW_Failed) - LW_Failed) / max_of(LW_Failed, 1) * 100, 0)
| project ThisWeek_Total = TW, LastWeek_Total = LW, VolumeChange, ThisWeek_Failed = TW_Failed, LastWeek_Failed = LW_Failed, FailureChange

name: Lateral Movement via NTLM
desc: Detects accounts authenticating to 3+ devices or 2+ devices within 30 minutes using NTLM. Pass-the-Hash attacks use NTLM for lateral movement. Requires MDE.
kql:
DeviceLogonEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where LogonType == "Network"
| where Protocol == "NTLM"
| summarize
    DeviceCount = dcount(DeviceName),
    Devices = make_set(DeviceName, 10),
    FirstLogon = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastLogon = max(TimeGenerated)
    by AccountName, AccountDomain
| where DeviceCount >= 3
| extend TimeSpan = datetime_diff('minute', LastLogon, FirstLogon)
| extend RiskLevel = case(
    DeviceCount >= 5, "CRITICAL - Wide Lateral Movement",
    TimeSpan < 30, "CRITICAL - Rapid Movement",
    "HIGH")
| order by DeviceCount desc

name: MITRE ATT&CK NTLM Techniques
desc: Detects MITRE techniques T1110.003 (Password Spraying), T1110.004 (Credential Stuffing) and T1550.002 (Pass the Hash) based on NTLM patterns.
kql:
DeviceLogonEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Protocol == "NTLM"
| where LogonType == "Network"
| summarize
    Devices = dcount(DeviceName),
    Attempts = count(),
    FailedAttempts = countif(ActionType == "LogonFailed")
    by AccountName
| extend MITRETechnique = case(
    Devices >= 3, "T1550.002 - Pass the Hash",
    FailedAttempts > 50, "T1110.003 - Password Spraying",
    Attempts > 100, "T1110.004 - Credential Stuffing",
    "Investigate")
| where MITRETechnique != "Investigate"
| order by Attempts desc

name: Threat Actor TTP (APT28/29, FIN7)
desc: Matches NTLM patterns against known APT28 (Fancy Bear), APT29 (Cozy Bear), FIN7 (Carbanak) and PetitPotam TTPs.
kql:
DeviceLogonEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Protocol == "NTLM"
| where LogonType == "Network"
| summarize
    Devices = dcount(DeviceName),
    Attempts = count(),
    UniqueAccounts = dcount(AccountName)
    by RemoteIP
| extend ThreatActor = case(
    Devices > 5 and UniqueAccounts > 3, "APT29/Cozy Bear Pattern - Domain Recon",
    Attempts > 100 and Devices > 3, "FIN7/Carbanak Pattern - AD Enumeration",
    UniqueAccounts > 10, "APT28/Fancy Bear Pattern - Credential Harvesting",
    "")
| where isnotempty(ThreatActor)
| order by Attempts desc

name: NTLMv1 Compliance Check
desc: Identifies any NTLMv1 authentication. NTLMv1 is cryptographically broken and must be zero. Any detection requires immediate remediation.
kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4624
| where LmPackageName has "NTLM V1"
| summarize
    NTLMv1Count = count(),
    Accounts = make_set(TargetUserName, 10),
    Computers = make_set(Computer, 10),
    SourceIPs = make_set(IpAddress, 5)
    by WorkstationName
| order by NTLMv1Count desc

name: External IPs - Successful NTLM
desc: Detects successful NTLM authentication from public/external IPs. NTLM should never be exposed to the internet.
kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4624
| where LogonType == 3
| where AuthenticationPackageName == "NTLM"
| where isnotempty(IpAddress) and IpAddress != "-" and IpAddress != "::1" and IpAddress != "127.0.0.1"
| where not(ipv4_is_private(IpAddress))
| summarize
    SuccessCount = count(),
    Accounts = make_set(TargetUserName, 5),
    Computers = make_set(Computer, 5)
    by IpAddress
| order by SuccessCount desc

name: NTLM Deprecation Progress
desc: Compares last 30 days NTLM usage against previous period to track deprecation progress and Kerberos migration effectiveness.
kql:
let Current = SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4624
| summarize NTLM_Current = countif(AuthenticationPackageName == "NTLM"), Kerberos_Current = countif(AuthenticationPackageName == "Kerberos");
let Previous = SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated between (ago(60d) .. ago(30d))
| where EventID == 4624
| summarize NTLM_Previous = countif(AuthenticationPackageName == "NTLM"), Kerberos_Previous = countif(AuthenticationPackageName == "Kerberos");
Current | extend p = 1 | join kind=leftouter (Previous | extend p = 1) on p
| extend NTLM_Change = round((todouble(NTLM_Current) - NTLM_Previous) / max_of(NTLM_Previous, 1) * 100, 0)
| project NTLM_Current, NTLM_Previous, NTLM_Change, Kerberos_Current, Kerberos_Previous

name: BloodHound/SharpHound Detection (MDI)
desc: Detects BloodHound/SharpHound collection via MDI: mass objectClass queries, memberOf enumeration and adminCount lookups. 500+ queries/hour = high confidence. Requires MDI.
kql:
let Patterns = dynamic(["objectClass=user", "objectClass=group", "objectClass=computer", "memberOf", "adminCount", "primaryGroupID", "servicePrincipalName"]);
IdentityDirectoryEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Protocol == "Ldap"
| where tostring(AdditionalFields) has_any (Patterns)
| summarize
    QueryCount = count(),
    Patterns = make_set(extract(@"(objectClass=[a-zA-Z]+|memberOf|adminCount|servicePrincipalName)", 0, tostring(AdditionalFields)), 10),
    FirstQuery = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastQuery = max(TimeGenerated)
    by IPAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where QueryCount > 10
| extend Confidence = case(QueryCount > 500, "CRITICAL - High Confidence SharpHound", QueryCount > 100, "HIGH - Likely SharpHound", "MEDIUM - Possible")
| order by QueryCount desc

name: BloodHound Detection (Event 1644)
desc: Same detection using Directory Service Event 1644 (expensive query log). Requires Field Engineering registry enabled on DCs. Works without MDI.
kql:
// NOTE: Requires Event 1644 enabled on DCs via registry:
// HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\Diagnostics\15 Field Engineering = 5
let Patterns = dynamic(["objectClass=user", "objectClass=group", "memberOf", "adminCount", "servicePrincipalName"]);
Event
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventLog == "Directory Service"
| where EventID == 1644
| where RenderedDescription has_any (Patterns)
| extend ClientIP = extract(@"Client IP:\s*([\d\.]+)", 1, RenderedDescription)
| extend Filter = extract(@"Filter:\s*(.+?)\s*(Visited|$)", 1, RenderedDescription)
| summarize QueryCount = count(), Filters = make_set(Filter, 5) by ClientIP, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where QueryCount > 10
| order by QueryCount desc

name: PowerView Enumeration (MDI)
desc: Detects PowerView patterns: samAccountType, userAccountControl and objectCategory queries. PowerView is the most common AD enumeration tool. Requires MDI.
kql:
let Patterns = dynamic(["samAccountType", "userAccountControl", "objectCategory", "lastLogon", "pwdLastSet", "servicePrincipalName", "805306368"]);
IdentityDirectoryEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Protocol == "Ldap"
| where tostring(AdditionalFields) has_any (Patterns)
| summarize
    QueryCount = count(),
    Patterns = make_set(extract(@"(samAccountType|userAccountControl|objectCategory|servicePrincipalName)", 1, tostring(AdditionalFields)), 10)
    by IPAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 10m)
| where QueryCount > 5
| extend Confidence = case(QueryCount > 100, "CRITICAL", QueryCount > 30, "HIGH", "MEDIUM")
| order by QueryCount desc

name: KRBTGT Account Access (Golden Ticket)
desc: Any LDAP query targeting KRBTGT is suspicious. Attackers query KRBTGT for Golden Ticket attacks granting unlimited domain access. Requires MDI.
kql:
IdentityDirectoryEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Protocol == "Ldap"
| where TargetAccountDisplayName has "krbtgt" or tostring(AdditionalFields) has "krbtgt"
| project TimeGenerated, AccountName, DeviceName, IPAddress, ActionType, AdditionalFields
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: AdminSDHolder Modification
desc: Detects modifications to AdminSDHolder container. This is a persistence technique — changes propagate to all protected objects every 60 minutes. Requires MDI.
kql:
IdentityDirectoryEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Protocol == "Ldap"
| where tostring(AdditionalFields) has "AdminSDHolder" or TargetAccountDisplayName has "AdminSDHolder"
| where ActionType has_any ("modified", "added", "deleted")
| project TimeGenerated, AccountName, DeviceName, IPAddress, ActionType, AdditionalFields
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: SPN Enumeration (Kerberoasting)
desc: Detects LDAP queries for servicePrincipalName attributes. Mass SPN enumeration is the first step of Kerberoasting — offline service account password cracking. Requires MDI.
kql:
IdentityDirectoryEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Protocol == "Ldap"
| where tostring(AdditionalFields) has "servicePrincipalName"
| summarize
    SPNQueries = count(),
    FirstQuery = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastQuery = max(TimeGenerated)
    by AccountName, DeviceName, IPAddress
| where SPNQueries > 3
| extend RiskLevel = case(SPNQueries > 20, "CRITICAL - Mass SPN Enumeration", SPNQueries > 10, "HIGH", "MEDIUM")
| order by SPNQueries desc

name: Certificate Template Enum (AD CS)
desc: Detects LDAP queries for certificate template attributes. Indicates preparation for AD CS attacks (ESC1 through ESC8). Requires MDI.
kql:
IdentityDirectoryEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Protocol == "Ldap"
| where tostring(AdditionalFields) has_any ("pKICertificateTemplate", "msPKI-Certificate-Name-Flag", "msPKI-Enrollment-Flag", "Cert Publishers")
| summarize
    CertQueries = count(),
    Templates = make_set(TargetAccountDisplayName, 10)
    by AccountName, DeviceName, IPAddress
| where CertQueries > 2
| order by CertQueries desc

name: Domain Trust Enumeration
desc: Detects LDAP queries for trust-related attributes. Attackers enumerate trusts for cross-forest attacks using SID History or Golden Tickets. Requires MDI.
kql:
IdentityDirectoryEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Protocol == "Ldap"
| where tostring(AdditionalFields) has_any ("trustedDomain", "trustDirection", "trustType", "trustAttributes")
| summarize TrustQueries = count() by AccountName, DeviceName, IPAddress
| where TrustQueries > 1
| order by TrustQueries desc

name: RBCD Configuration Queries
desc: Detects queries for msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity. RBCD abuse allows impersonating any user to a target service. Requires MDI.
kql:
IdentityDirectoryEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Protocol == "Ldap"
| where tostring(AdditionalFields) has "msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity"
| project TimeGenerated, AccountName, DeviceName, IPAddress, ActionType, TargetAccountDisplayName
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Expensive LDAP Queries (Event 1644)
desc: Captures LDAP queries visiting 100+ entries or taking 500+ ms. Identifies both performance issues and recon tools generating heavy directory scans.
kql:
// NOTE: Requires Event 1644 enabled via registry on DCs
Event
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventLog == "Directory Service"
| where EventID == 1644
| extend ClientIP = extract(@"Client IP:\s*([\d\.]+)", 1, RenderedDescription)
| extend Filter = extract(@"Filter:\s*(.+?)\s*(Visited|$)", 1, RenderedDescription)
| extend VisitedEntries = toint(extract(@"Visited entries:\s*(\d+)", 1, RenderedDescription))
| extend TimeMs = toint(extract(@"(\d+)\s*ms", 1, RenderedDescription))
| where VisitedEntries > 100 or TimeMs > 500
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, ClientIP, Filter, VisitedEntries, TimeMs
| order by VisitedEntries desc
| take 50

name: Unsigned LDAP Bind (Event 2889)
desc: Detects clients performing unsigned LDAP simple binds. Unsigned binds expose credentials in transit. Enforce LDAP signing (LDAPServerIntegrity=2).
kql:
Event
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventLog == "Directory Service"
| where EventID == 2889
| extend ClientIP = extract(@"IP address:\s*([\d\.]+)", 1, RenderedDescription)
| summarize
    UnsignedBinds = count(),
    UniqueClients = dcount(ClientIP),
    Clients = make_set(ClientIP, 10)
    by Computer
| order by UnsignedBinds desc

name: LDAP Relay Attack Indicators
desc: Correlates successful NTLM network logons to DCs with recent failed attempts. Pattern indicates NTLM relay attacks against domain controllers.
kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4624
| where LogonType == 3
| where AuthenticationPackageName == "NTLM"
| join kind=inner (
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4625
    | project FailedTime = TimeGenerated, FailedAccount = Account, FailedIP = IpAddress
) on $left.Account == $right.FailedAccount
| where (TimeGenerated - FailedTime) between (-5m .. 5m)
| summarize RelayIndicators = count(), SuccessAfterFailure = countif(TimeGenerated > FailedTime) by Account, IpAddress
| where RelayIndicators > 2
| order by RelayIndicators desc

name: LDAP Security Posture Score
desc: Calculates a 0-100 security score based on unsigned binds, clear text binds, expensive queries and privileged group recon.

name: SP Impossible Travel
desc: Detects service principals authenticating from geographically impossible locations. Indicates stolen SP credentials used from attacker infrastructure.
kql:
AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| extend Country = tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).countryOrRegion)
| extend City = tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).city)
| where isnotempty(Country)
| summarize
    Countries = make_set(Country, 10),
    CountryCount = dcount(Country),
    IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 10),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by ServicePrincipalName, ServicePrincipalId
| extend TimeSpan = datetime_diff('minute', LastSeen, FirstSeen)
| where CountryCount >= 2 and TimeSpan <= 60
| order by CountryCount desc

name: SP Sign-In from New Countries
desc: Compares SP sign-in countries against 30-day baseline. SPs should have consistent geographic patterns — a new country is a strong compromise indicator.
kql:
let Baseline = AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated between (ago(30d) .. ago(7d))
| where ResultType == "0"
| extend Country = tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).countryOrRegion)
| distinct ServicePrincipalId, Country;
AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| extend Country = tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).countryOrRegion)
| join kind=leftanti (Baseline) on ServicePrincipalId, Country
| summarize Events = count(), IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 5) by ServicePrincipalName, Country
| order by Events desc

name: SP Credential Changes
desc: Tracks all service principal credential additions, removals and updates. Unauthorized credential changes indicate attacker adding persistence.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName has_any ("Add service principal credentials", "Remove service principal credentials", "Update application – Certificates and secrets management")
| extend SPName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend ModifiedBy = coalesce(tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName), tostring(InitiatedBy.app.displayName))
| extend ModifiedByIP = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.ipAddress)
| project TimeGenerated, OperationName, SPName, ModifiedBy, ModifiedByIP, Result
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Unused SPs (90+ Days Inactive)
desc: Compares all registered SPs against 90-day sign-in activity. Inactive SPs are attack surface — stolen credentials go unnoticed. 180+ days = consider removal.
kql:
let ActiveSPs = AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
| distinct ServicePrincipalId;
let AllSPs = AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(365d)
| where OperationName in ("Add service principal", "Update service principal")
| extend SPName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend SPId = tostring(TargetResources[0].id)
| extend CreatedBy = coalesce(tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName), tostring(InitiatedBy.app.displayName))
| summarize FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastAuditEvent = max(TimeGenerated), CreatedBy = take_any(CreatedBy) by SPName, SPId;
AllSPs
| join kind=leftanti (ActiveSPs) on $left.SPId == $right.ServicePrincipalId
| extend DaysInactive = datetime_diff('day', now(), LastAuditEvent)
| extend RiskLevel = case(DaysInactive > 180, "HIGH - Consider Removal", DaysInactive > 90, "MEDIUM - Review Required", "LOW")
| order by DaysInactive desc

name: Botnet: Multiple SPs Same IP
desc: Detects 3+ different service principals authenticating from the same IP. Indicates stolen credentials being tested from C2 infrastructure.
kql:
AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where IPAddress != ''
| summarize
    UniqueSPs = dcount(ServicePrincipalId),
    SPNames = make_set(ServicePrincipalName, 10),
    TotalAttempts = count(),
    FailedAttempts = countif(ResultType != '0')
    by IPAddress
| where UniqueSPs >= 3
| extend RiskLevel = case(UniqueSPs >= 10, "CRITICAL - Botnet C2", UniqueSPs >= 5, "HIGH", "MEDIUM")
| order by UniqueSPs desc

name: Managed Identity New Resource Access
desc: Compares current MI resource access against 30-day baseline. A managed identity accessing a new resource could indicate compromised Azure workload.
kql:
let Baseline = AADManagedIdentitySignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated between (ago(30d) .. ago(7d))
| where ResultType == "0"
| distinct ServicePrincipalId, ResourceDisplayName;
AADManagedIdentitySignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| join kind=leftanti (Baseline) on ServicePrincipalId, ResourceDisplayName
| project TimeGenerated, ServicePrincipalName, ServicePrincipalId, ResourceDisplayName
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: SP Without Conditional Access
desc: Lists service principals authenticating without any Conditional Access policy. These SPs lack geo-fencing and IP restrictions.
kql:
let SPWithCA = AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ConditionalAccessStatus in ("success", "failure")
| distinct ServicePrincipalId;
AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| join kind=leftanti (SPWithCA) on ServicePrincipalId
| summarize SignInCount = count(), UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress), Resources = make_set(ResourceDisplayName, 5) by ServicePrincipalName
| order by SignInCount desc

name: High-Risk OAuth Permissions
desc: Detects consent grants for dangerous permissions: Mail.Read, Mail.Send, Files.ReadWrite.All, Directory.ReadWrite.All. These enable full mailbox and file access.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName == "Consent to application"
| extend ConsentType = tostring(TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties[0].newValue)
| extend AppName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend GrantedBy = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| where ConsentType has_any ("Mail.Read", "Mail.ReadWrite", "Mail.Send", "Files.ReadWrite.All", "Directory.ReadWrite.All", "full_access_as_app")
| project TimeGenerated, AppName, GrantedBy, ConsentType
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Illicit Consent Grant Detection
desc: Identifies consent grants to suspicious applications. Illicit consent grant is a common attack where users are phished into granting app access.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName == "Consent to application"
| extend AppName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend Permissions = tostring(TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties)
| extend ConsentUser = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| extend IsSuspicious = case(
    Permissions has_any ("Mail.Read", "Mail.Send", "Files.ReadWrite.All", "full_access"), true,
    AppName has_any ("test", "demo", "oauth", "token"), true,
    false)
| where IsSuspicious == true
| project TimeGenerated, ConsentUser, AppName, Permissions
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Multi-Tenant OAuth Applications
desc: Detects apps configured as multi-tenant (AzureADMultipleOrgs). Multi-tenant apps can be accessed by any Azure AD tenant — increases external attack surface.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName has_any ("Add application", "Update application")
| extend ModifiedProps = parse_json(tostring(TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties))
| mv-expand ModifiedProps
| where tostring(ModifiedProps.displayName) has_any ("AvailableToOtherTenants", "SignInAudience")
| where tostring(ModifiedProps.newValue) has_any ("AzureADMultipleOrgs", "AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount", "true")
| extend AppName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend ModifiedBy = coalesce(tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName), tostring(InitiatedBy.app.displayName))
| project TimeGenerated, AppName, ModifiedBy
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Graph API Permission Analysis
desc: Analyzes all Microsoft Graph API permission grants categorized by risk: Mail, Files, Directory, User, Calendar, Teams and Application permissions.
kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName has_any ("Add app role assignment to service principal", "Add OAuth2PermissionGrant", "Consent to application")
| extend AppName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend PropValue = tostring(TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties)
| where PropValue has_any ("Microsoft Graph", "graph.microsoft.com")
| extend GrantedBy = coalesce(tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName), tostring(InitiatedBy.app.displayName))
| extend RiskLevel = case(
    PropValue has_any ("Mail.ReadWrite", "Mail.Send", "Directory.ReadWrite.All"), "HIGH-RISK",
    PropValue has_any ("Mail.Read", "Files.Read.All", "User.Read.All"), "SENSITIVE",
    "STANDARD")
| project TimeGenerated, AppName, GrantedBy, RiskLevel, PropValue
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: MITRE TTP - Non-Human Identity
desc: Maps non-human identity events to MITRE ATT&CK: T1098.001 (Additional Cloud Credentials), T1098.003 (Additional Cloud Roles), T1136.003 (Cloud Account Creation).
kql:
union
    (AuditLogs | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where OperationName has_any ("Add service principal credentials", "Update application – Certificates") | extend TTP = "T1098.001", Technique = "Additional Cloud Credentials"),
    (AuditLogs | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where OperationName has_any ("Add member to role", "Add app role assignment") | where tostring(TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties) has_any ("Global Administrator", "Exchange Administrator") | extend TTP = "T1098.003", Technique = "Additional Cloud Roles"),
    (AuditLogs | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where OperationName in ("Add application", "Add service principal") | extend TTP = "T1136.003", Technique = "Cloud Account Creation")
| extend Details = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| project TimeGenerated, TTP, Technique, OperationName, Details
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Domain Impersonation (BEC)
desc: Detects emails flagged by anti-phishing domain impersonation policies. Shows sender details, recipient targets and delivery actions.
kql:
EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EmailActionPolicy has_any ("Anti-phishing", "domain impersonation", "impersonation")
| summarize Count = count(), UniqueRecipients = dcount(RecipientEmailAddress), Subjects = make_set(Subject, 5)
    by SenderFromAddress, SenderFromDomain, SenderDisplayName, DeliveryAction
| order by Count desc

name: BEC Financial Keywords
desc: Hunts for inbound emails with financial keywords (urgent, wire, payment, invoice, bank, ACH, payroll) that reached mailboxes.
kql:
// NOTE: Adjust keywords based on your organization's terminology
let Keywords = dynamic(["urgent", "wire", "transfer", "payment", "invoice", "bank", "account", "confidential", "immediate", "asap", "w-2", "payroll", "ach", "direct deposit"]);
EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EmailDirection == "Inbound"
| where tolower(Subject) has_any (Keywords)
| summarize Count = count(), DeliveredCount = countif(DeliveryAction == "Delivered"), Subjects = make_set(Subject, 5)
    by SenderFromAddress, SenderFromDomain
| where Count > 1
| order by DeliveredCount desc

name: Display Name Spoofing (Executive)
desc: Detects external senders using display names matching executive titles from non-internal domains. Classic CEO fraud technique.
kql:
// NOTE: Replace domain.com with your actual domain
let InternalDomains = dynamic(["domain.com"]);
EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EmailDirection == "Inbound"
| where tolower(SenderDisplayName) has_any ("ceo", "cfo", "cto", "ciso", "president", "director", "vp", "chief", "executive")
| where not(SenderFromDomain has_any (InternalDomains))
| summarize Count = count(), DeliveredCount = countif(DeliveryAction == "Delivered"), Domains = make_set(SenderFromDomain, 5)
    by SenderDisplayName
| where Count > 1
| order by DeliveredCount desc

name: Account Compromise - Outbound Blast
desc: Identifies internal accounts sending at unusually high rates (>20/hour) or to many external recipients (>50). Indicates compromised account.
kql:
EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EmailDirection == "Outbound"
| summarize
    TotalSent = count(),
    UniqueRecipients = dcount(RecipientEmailAddress),
    ExternalRecipients = dcountif(RecipientEmailAddress, not(RecipientEmailAddress has SenderFromDomain)),
    UniqueHours = dcount(bin(TimeGenerated, 1h))
    by SenderFromAddress
| extend EmailsPerHour = round(toreal(TotalSent) / max_of(UniqueHours, 1), 2)
| where EmailsPerHour > 20 or ExternalRecipients > 50
| order by EmailsPerHour desc

name: AiTM: Phishing → Sign-in Correlation
desc: Correlates users who received delivered phishing emails with successful sign-ins within 24 hours. <30 min = high-confidence AiTM compromise.
kql:
let PhishRecipients = EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ThreatTypes has "Phish" and DeliveryAction in ("Delivered", "Junked")
| summarize PhishEmails = count(), LastPhish = max(TimeGenerated) by RecipientEmailAddress;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| join kind=inner (PhishRecipients) on $left.UserPrincipalName == $right.RecipientEmailAddress
| where TimeGenerated > LastPhish and datetime_diff('hour', TimeGenerated, LastPhish) <= 24
| extend Country = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion)
| extend MinutesAfterPhish = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, LastPhish)
| summarize SignIns = count(), Countries = make_set(Country, 5), AvgMinutes = avg(MinutesAfterPhish)
    by UserPrincipalName, PhishEmails
| extend Risk = case(AvgMinutes < 30, "CRITICAL", AvgMinutes < 60, "HIGH", "MEDIUM")
| order by AvgMinutes asc

name: URL Click → Sign-in Correlation
desc: Joins users who clicked through malicious URLs with successful sign-ins within 60 minutes. Multi-country = almost certain credential theft.
kql:
let ClickedUsers = UrlClickEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where IsClickedThrough == true
| where ThreatTypes has_any ("Phish", "Malware")
| summarize ClickCount = count(), LastClick = max(TimeGenerated), URLs = make_set(Url, 3) by AccountUpn
| project UserPrincipalName = AccountUpn, ClickCount, LastClick, URLs;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| join kind=inner ClickedUsers on UserPrincipalName
| where TimeGenerated > LastClick
| where datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, LastClick) <= 60
| extend Country = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion)
| summarize SignIns = count(), Countries = make_set(Country, 5), IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 5)
    by UserPrincipalName, ClickCount
| extend RiskLevel = case(SignIns > 5, "CRITICAL", array_length(Countries) > 1, "CRITICAL", "HIGH")
| order by SignIns desc

name: AiTM Toolkit URLs (Evilginx)
desc: Scans email URLs for known AiTM/phishing toolkit patterns: evilginx, modlishka, muraena, gophish and suspicious login-like patterns.
kql:
let Patterns = dynamic(["evilginx", "modlishka", "muraena", "gophish", "credsniper", "-login", "login-", "signin-", "auth-", "verify-", "secure-"]);
EmailUrlInfo
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where tolower(Url) has_any (Patterns) or tolower(UrlDomain) has_any (Patterns)
| join kind=inner (EmailEvents | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | project NetworkMessageId, RecipientEmailAddress, SenderFromAddress, Subject, DeliveryAction) on NetworkMessageId
| summarize Count = count(), DeliveredCount = countif(DeliveryAction == "Delivered"), Recipients = make_set(RecipientEmailAddress, 10) by UrlDomain
| order by DeliveredCount desc

name: Delivered Phishing Investigation
desc: Lists all emails classified as phishing that were delivered to mailboxes. Each result requires investigation.
kql:
EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ThreatTypes has "Phish"
| where DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
| project TimeGenerated, RecipientEmailAddress, SenderFromAddress, SenderDisplayName, Subject, SenderFromDomain, DeliveryLocation, NetworkMessageId
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

name: Malware Attack Sources
desc: Identifies top sender domains delivering malware. Highlights any malware that reached mailboxes (delivered) vs properly blocked.
kql:
EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ThreatTypes has "Malware"
| summarize Count = count(), Blocked = countif(DeliveryAction in ("Blocked", "Quarantined")), Delivered = countif(DeliveryAction == "Delivered"), Recipients = dcount(RecipientEmailAddress)
    by SenderFromDomain, SenderFromAddress
| order by Delivered desc, Count desc
| take 20

name: Malicious URL Clickers
desc: Lists users who clicked through Safe Links warnings. These users need immediate follow-up: password reset, session revocation and endpoint scan.
kql:
UrlClickEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where IsClickedThrough == true
| where isnotempty(ThreatTypes)
| summarize ClickCount = count(), URLs = make_set(Url, 5), Workloads = make_set(Workload, 3)
    by AccountUpn, ThreatTypes
| order by ClickCount desc

name: SPF/DKIM/DMARC Failures
desc: Analyzes email authentication failures by sender domain. DMARC fail + delivered = spoofing risk. Helps identify domains needing enforcement.
kql:
EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EmailDirection == "Inbound"
| where AuthenticationDetails has_any ("fail", "none", "softfail")
| extend SPF = extract(@"spf=([a-z]+)", 1, tolower(AuthenticationDetails))
| extend DKIM = extract(@"dkim=([a-z]+)", 1, tolower(AuthenticationDetails))
| extend DMARC = extract(@"dmarc=([a-z]+)", 1, tolower(AuthenticationDetails))
| where SPF in ("fail", "softfail") or DMARC == "fail"
| summarize FailCount = count(), DeliveredCount = countif(DeliveryAction == "Delivered") by SenderFromDomain, SPF, DKIM, DMARC
| where FailCount > 5
| order by DeliveredCount desc

name: ZAP Post-Delivery Actions
desc: Tracks Zero-Hour Auto Purge activity: success/failure rates, threat types and affected users. Validates ZAP is working correctly.
kql:
EmailPostDeliveryEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize Count = count(), SuccessCount = countif(ActionResult == "Success"), Recipients = dcount(RecipientEmailAddress)
    by Action, ActionTrigger, ThreatTypes
| extend SuccessRate = round(toreal(SuccessCount) / Count * 100, 1)
| order by Count desc

name: External Forwarding Rules
desc: Detects inbox rules forwarding to external addresses. Most common post-compromise persistence technique.
kql:
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Operation in ("New-InboxRule", "Set-InboxRule", "Set-Mailbox")
| where Parameters has_any ("ForwardTo", "ForwardAsAttachmentTo", "RedirectTo", "ForwardingSmtpAddress")
| extend Target = extract(@"(ForwardTo|RedirectTo|ForwardingSmtpAddress)[^@]*@([\w.-]+)", 2, tostring(Parameters))
| where isnotempty(Target)
| project TimeGenerated, UserId, Operation, Target
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Teams Safe Links Analysis
desc: Analyzes URL clicks within Teams, showing blocked vs clicked-through threats. Teams is increasingly targeted.
kql:
UrlClickEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Workload == "Teams"
| where isnotempty(ThreatTypes) or ActionType has "Blocked"
| summarize ThreatClicks = count(), BlockedClicks = countif(ActionType has "Blocked"), ClickedThrough = countif(IsClickedThrough == true)
    by AccountUpn
| order by ClickedThrough desc

name: Mass File Operations (Ransomware)
desc: Detects mass file deletions/renames/downloads (>50/hour) in SharePoint/OneDrive. Combined delete+rename = strong ransomware indicator.
kql:
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OfficeWorkload in ("SharePoint", "OneDrive")
| where Operation has_any ("FileDeleted", "FileRenamed", "FileDownloaded")
| summarize OpCount = count(), DeleteCount = countif(Operation has "Deleted"), RenameCount = countif(Operation has "Renamed"), DownloadCount = countif(Operation has "Downloaded")
    by UserId, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where OpCount > 50
| extend Indicator = case(DeleteCount > 30 and RenameCount > 30, "RANSOMWARE", DownloadCount > 100, "EXFILTRATION", "HIGH VOLUME")
| order by OpCount desc

name: External/Guest User Activity
desc: Monitors external/guest user file operations across Teams, SharePoint and OneDrive. Risk scored by operation type.
kql:
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OfficeWorkload in ("SharePoint", "OneDrive", "MicrosoftTeams")
| where UserId contains "#ext#" or UserId contains "urn:" or ExternalAccess == true
| summarize EventCount = count(), FileAccess = countif(Operation has_any ("Accessed", "Downloaded", "Viewed")), FileModify = countif(Operation has_any ("Modified", "Uploaded"))
    by UserId
| extend RiskScore = FileAccess + (FileModify * 2)
| order by RiskScore desc
| take 20

name: Risky File Types Upload
desc: Detects uploads of executable/script file types to SharePoint, OneDrive and Teams. These files should not normally be in collaboration platforms.
kql:
let Risky = dynamic([".exe", ".bat", ".cmd", ".ps1", ".vbs", ".js", ".hta", ".scr", ".msi", ".dll", ".iso", ".img"]);
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OfficeWorkload in ("SharePoint", "OneDrive", "MicrosoftTeams")
| where Operation has_any ("FileUploaded", "FileCreated")
| extend Ext = tolower(extract(@"\.[a-zA-Z0-9]+$", 0, SourceFileName))
| where Ext in (Risky)
| summarize Count = count(), Users = make_set(UserId, 5) by Ext, SourceFileName
| order by Count desc

name: Multi-Stage Attack Chain (Email → Cred)
desc: Correlates email security alerts with identity alerts for the same user within 72 hours. Finds complete phishing → credential theft → takeover chains.
kql:
let EmailAlerts = AlertInfo | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where ServiceSource has_any ("Office 365", "MDO", "Microsoft 365 Defender") | project AlertId, EmailTime = TimeGenerated, EmailTitle = Title;
let IdAlerts = AlertInfo | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where ServiceSource has_any ("Azure AD", "Microsoft Entra", "MCAS") | project AlertId, IdTime = TimeGenerated, IdTitle = Title;
AlertEvidence | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where isnotempty(AccountUpn) | project AlertId, AccountUpn
| join kind=inner EmailAlerts on AlertId
| join kind=inner (AlertEvidence | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where isnotempty(AccountUpn) | project IdAlertId = AlertId, AccountUpn | join kind=inner IdAlerts on $left.IdAlertId == $right.AlertId) on AccountUpn
| where IdTime > EmailTime and IdTime < EmailTime + 72h
| summarize EmailAlerts = make_set(EmailTitle, 5), IdAlerts = make_set(IdTitle, 5), TimeToCompromise = min(datetime_diff('minute', IdTime, EmailTime)) by AccountUpn
| order by TimeToCompromise asc

name: Phishing → Risky Sign-In Correlation
desc: Joins delivered phishing emails with risky sign-in events within 24 hours. Shows average time-to-compromise and source IPs.
kql:
let SuspiciousEmails = EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ThreatTypes has "Phish" and DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
| project RecipientEmailAddress, EmailTime = TimeGenerated;
union SigninLogs, AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where RiskLevelDuringSignIn in ("medium", "high")
| join kind=inner SuspiciousEmails on $left.UserPrincipalName == $right.RecipientEmailAddress
| where TimeGenerated > EmailTime and TimeGenerated < EmailTime + 24h
| extend MinutesToCompromise = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, EmailTime)
| summarize RiskySignIns = count(), AvgMinutes = avg(MinutesToCompromise), IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 5)
    by UserPrincipalName
| order by RiskySignIns desc

name: Post-Compromise Inbox Rules Alerts
desc: Pulls XDR alerts for suspicious inbox rules, mail forwarding and email exfiltration. Post-compromise indicator.
kql:
AlertInfo
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Title has_any ("inbox rule", "forwarding rule", "mail forwarding", "redirect", "auto-forward")
| join kind=leftouter (AlertEvidence | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | summarize Users = make_set(AccountUpn, 10), Targets = make_set(RemoteUrl, 10) by AlertId) on AlertId
| project TimeGenerated, Severity, Title, Users, Targets, ServiceSource
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Combined Email + Identity Alert Users
desc: Identifies users appearing in both email and identity alerts. Both domains = high-confidence compromise requiring immediate response.

name: Tagged Incidents (BEC/AiTM/Credential)
desc: Pulls Sentinel incidents tagged with BEC, AiTM or credential phishing labels. Filtered view of most critical identity-related incidents. Sentinel-only.
kql:
// NOTE: This query works in Sentinel only (SecurityIncident table)
SecurityIncident
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Labels has_any ("BEC", "AiTM", "Credential", "Phish", "fraud")
    or Title has_any ("BEC", "AiTM", "credential phish", "token theft")
| extend OwnerName = tostring(Owner.assignedTo)
| extend AlertCount = array_length(AlertIds)
| project TimeGenerated, IncidentNumber, Severity, Status, Title, OwnerName, AlertCount
| order by TimeGenerated desc

name: Trusted vs Untrusted Location
desc: Compares sign-in IPs against trusted named locations and known Azure IP ranges. Activity from untrusted locations needs investigation.
kql:
// NOTE: Update TrustedCountries with your organization's trusted countries
let TrustedCountries = dynamic(["United States", "Canada", "United Kingdom", "Germany", "France", "Netherlands"]);
union
    (AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | extend IdentityType = "Service Principal", IdentityName = ServicePrincipalName),
    (AADManagedIdentitySignInLogs | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | extend IdentityType = "Managed Identity", IdentityName = ServicePrincipalName)
| extend Country = tostring(geo_info_from_ip_address(IPAddress).country)
| extend IsTrusted = ipv4_is_private(IPAddress) or Country in (TrustedCountries)
| where IsTrusted == false
| summarize Events = count(), Identities = make_set(IdentityName, 5), SuccessCount = countif(ResultType == "0") by IPAddress, Country
| order by SuccessCount desc

name: Threat IP → Sign-in Detection
desc: Cross-references email threat source IPs with sign-in logs. If a user signs in from the same IP that sent phishing email, the account is likely compromised.
kql:
let ThreatIPs = EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where isnotempty(ThreatTypes) and isnotempty(SenderIPv4)
| distinct SenderIPv4;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where IPAddress in (ThreatIPs)
| extend Country = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion)
| summarize SignInCount = count(), UniqueUsers = dcount(UserPrincipalName), Users = make_set(UserPrincipalName, 5), SuccessCount = countif(ResultType == "0")
    by IPAddress, Country
| order by SuccessCount desc
